Henry M. Stephens, attorney for the
Northern Pacific, has been at the city
hall urging the officials to take up
and put through the plan for building
the viaduct under the dangerous rail-
road crossing at Sprague avenue and
Division street, and the board of pub
lie works are earnestly considering the
proposition of Mr.. Stephens, together
with plan to build under the rail
road tracks ~ Brown street and close
up the crossing at Sprague avenue and
Division street. 1t is likely meeting
of city council as a whole will be
called within the next few days to aid
In the discussion.


Commissioner Handlev, in speaking of
the plans for doing away with the
Sprague. avenue-Division street peril to
life anF limb said this morning:

gested by the railroad company would
mean per cent grade, an excavation
of at least 81 feet under the present
grade level, and the consequent altera-
tons to heavy water and sewer pipes
that now run down Sprague avenue into
the city. 1t would mean also that
